matches.match_rationale: CVs are unstructured text containing key qualification signals — certifications, degrees, years of experience, subject specializations, and teaching registrations. Extracting these attributes automatically from hundreds of applications eliminates the manual read-through that creates the 2-4 week bottleneck. For a multi-campus education operator with no ATS, this is the foundational digitization step that enables all downstream screening logic.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI layer that parses uploaded CVs and extracts structured qualification fields (teaching license, subject area, years of experience, location, prior institution type) into a unified candidate record, replacing manual data entry across 10-15 campuses.

matches.match_rationale: Once CV text is available, a classifier can score each application against a defined rubric — qualified, underqualified, or needs review — replacing inconsistent human judgments across campuses. This directly addresses the duplicate effort and inconsistent qualification standards pain: the same model applies the same rubric everywhere, instantly. Multi-label classification can also flag specific disqualifiers (no registration, wrong subject area) without human reading.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A qualification-tier classifier trained or prompted on the operator's hiring criteria that auto-tags each CV as Pass/Review/Reject with a reason code, feeding a shared dashboard visible across all campuses simultaneously.

matches.match_rationale: The full screening workflow — receiving applications, parsing CVs, scoring against rubrics, deduplicating candidates who applied to multiple campuses, routing shortlists to the right campus coordinator — is a multi-step process that currently requires manual coordination across 10-15 sites. An automation agent can orchestrate this end-to-end, replacing the fragmented email/spreadsheet workflow that creates the 2-4 week delay. This is the capability that compresses the full pipeline rather than just one step.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agent-driven applicant processing pipeline that ingests applications from a central intake form, runs extraction and classification, deduplicates cross-campus applicants, and delivers a ranked shortlist per campus — reducing time-to-shortlist from weeks to hours.

matches.match_rationale: Beyond initial screening, allocating shortlisted candidates across campuses is a matching problem with hard constraints: a candidate's location, subject qualification, and availability must match a campus's open role and subject need. With 10-15 campuses competing for a thin provincial talent pool, naive assignment causes duplication and missed placements. Constraint satisfaction finds valid assignments that maximize overall coverage given hard qualification requirements and soft preferences.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A candidate-to-campus matching tool that takes the screened shortlist and assigns candidates to interview slots at the most suitable campus, respecting qualification fit, travel feasibility, and campus priority — preventing two campuses from pursuing the same candidate independently.
